title: The Governance of England
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML is a copy of University of Michigan CME item AEW3422.0001.001. Its immediate print source is Charles Plummer's revised 1885 edition, but the XML verbally encodes only the retained carrier title page and Plummer's target text on printed pages 109-157: twenty numbered chapters, not the edition's introduction, apparatus, notes, or back matter. Plummer used Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 593 as base through Chapter XIX at printed p. 155, line 27 and supplied the lost ending from British Library Cotton MS Claudius A VIII; he collated ten witnesses in all. Earlier editions of 1714 and 1869 belong to a separate print branch. Complete scans of the three editions and a complete 372-canvas facsimile of Digby 145 are cached; no public complete facsimile of the other nine witnesses was verified on 2026-07-15.
review_notes: The abstract medieval work, its manuscript witnesses, three printed editions, U-M encoding, repository copy, and modern facsimiles are separate entities.

The XML's 412 PB milestones preserve the complete scanned carrier sequence, but only 50 BODY PB milestones correspond to the target's printed pp. 109-157 plus one leading blank; omitted front and back matter are page-image scaffolding, not transcribed work content.
The retained title page is zero-count carrier paratext and is not one of the work's twenty chapters.
Plummer's ten-witness collation is distinct from the two passage-scoped transcription bases.
The 1714 print branch is routed through Thomas Hearne's c. 1698-1699 working transcript; a direct 1714-to-Digby 145 transcription edge would flatten the documentary evidence.

Rawlinson B.384 and CUL Ii.3.11 are routed through Plummer's unidentified shared text taken from Digby 145. CUL's additional Y-type material is routed through a second unidentified source and is not equated with surviving Add MS 48031 A.

Seven exact official catalog deliverables are cached as metadata-only files: three pinned Bodleian TEI records, three British Library JSON records, and one Lambeth RDF/XML record. Each is marked target_work_presence absent because descriptive metadata is not a facsimile or transcription.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aew3422
entities.description: Exact XML artifact consumed by General Corpus and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AEW3422.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 Level 1 TEI encoding produced from uncorrected OCR with linked page images. It retains the carrier title page and encodes Plummer's work text on pp. 109-157; omitted front and back matter remain represented only by PB milestones.
notes: Source description records 412 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images and no OCR corrections.

entities.label: Sir John Fortescue, The Governance of England
alternate_labels: The Difference between an Absolute and a Limited Monarchy
Sir John Fortescue on the Governance of England
entities.description: A twenty-chapter Middle English political and constitutional treatise addressed in its surviving form to royal governance, counsel, finance, and the contrast between regal and political dominion.

entities.label: Charles Plummer's revised edition of The Governance of England (1885)
entities.description: Historically oriented edition in which the target occupies pp. 109-157. Laud Misc. 593 supplies the base through p. 155, line 27; Cotton Claudius A VIII supplies the remainder. Plummer collated ten witnesses and records selected variants.

bibliographic.citation: Charles Plummer, ed., The Governance of England: Otherwise Called The Difference between an Absolute and a Limited Monarchy (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1885), 387 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Governance of England: Otherwise Called The Difference between an Absolute and a Limited Monarchy

entities.label: John Fortescue-Aland's edition of The Difference between an Absolute and a Limited Monarchy (1714)
entities.description: First printed edition, based on Sir Adrian Fortescue's Digby 145 through Thomas Hearne's transcript and collated with Laud Misc. 593, Digby 198, and Cotton Claudius A VIII.

bibliographic.citation: John Fortescue-Aland, ed., The Difference between an Absolute and a Limited Monarchy (London: W. Bowyer, 1714).
bibliographic.title: The Difference between an Absolute and a Limited Monarchy

entities.label: Thomas Fortescue, Lord Clermont's edition in The Works of Sir John Fortescue, vol. 1 (1869)
entities.description: Reprints the 1714 edition on pp. 449-474 after re-collation with Lambeth MS 262 and Harley MS 542; its introduction on pp. 445-447 documents the 1714 base and collations.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Fortescue, Lord Clermont, ed., The Works of Sir John Fortescue, vol. 1 (London, 1869), pp. 445-474.
bibliographic.title: The Works of Sir John Fortescue

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 593
entities.description: Plummer's principal base manuscript. His edition dates it circa 1480-1490; the current Bodleian catalog dates it 1500-1510. The copy is mutilated in Chapter XIX at the point represented on edition p. 155, line 27.
notes: Plummer siglum L. Its chapter division is unique among the witnesses he collated.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Claudius A VIII
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century witness of Sir John Fortescue's De dominio regale et politico at current ff. 175-197. Plummer used it both in collation and to supply the ending missing from Laud Misc. 593.

date_statements.label: Catalog date
date_statements.value: Second half of the fifteenth century

notes: Plummer siglum C.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 48031 A (Yelverton MS 35)
entities.description: Witness of the target at current ff. 148b-164, cataloged 1471-1476. Plummer describes a freely expanded and altered version, with the title Sir John Fortescue on the Governance of England.
notes: Plummer siglum Y; formerly Yelverton MS 35.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 542
entities.description: John Stow's late-sixteenth-century autograph transcript of the target at ff. 125r-140r, copied from Add MS 48031 A.

date_statements.label: Copying
date_statements.value: Fourth quarter of the sixteenth century

notes: Plummer siglum H1. The current record's legacy digital-viewer link states that digital images are currently unavailable.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library, MS 262
entities.description: Early-sixteenth-century witness at ff. 106r-128v. It ends with finis after Chapter XVIII and lacks Chapters XIX-XX.
notes: Plummer siglum Lb.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Digby 198
entities.description: Late-fifteenth-century paper witness, target item 2. Plummer records ff. 48-75, an ending after Chapter XVIII, and later Protestant alterations.
notes: Plummer siglum D1.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 1757
entities.description: Mid-sixteenth-century witness at ff. 196-203 that stops in Chapter XV because its scribe ceased copying, rather than through later physical mutilation.

holding.notes: The exact current BL catalog hierarchy and direct-record sequence checked on 2026-07-15 did not resolve a public item page; this is a dated search result, not a universal claim that no online record exists.

notes: Plummer siglum H2.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Digby 145
entities.description: Complete witness copied by Sir Adrian Fortescue in 1532, target ff. 131v-159ar. A complete 372-canvas Digital Bodleian facsimile is public.
notes: Plummer siglum D2.

entities.label: Unidentified Digby 145-derived shared exemplar of Rawlinson B.384 and CUL Ii.3.11
entities.description: A lost or unidentified textual intermediary inferred by Plummer because Rawlinson B.384 and CUL Ii.3.11 repeatedly agree in departures from Digby 145 while each has lacunae absent from the other. Plummer concludes that both were copied from a text taken from Digby 145.
notes: This is a stemmatically inferred intermediary, not a known surviving manuscript, and it must not be collapsed into Digby 145 itself.

entities.label: Unidentified Y-type source used by the CUL Ii.3.11 copyist
entities.description: An unidentified text of the Yelverton textual type inferred by Plummer from material and chapter titles entered in CUL Ii.3.11 in addition to its Digby-derived base.
notes: This source is not equated with surviving Add MS 48031 A/Yelverton MS 35. Y-type denotes textual affinity only.

entities.label: Thomas Hearne's working transcript of Digby 145 (c. 1698-1699)
entities.description: A working transcript made by Thomas Hearne from Sir Adrian Fortescue's Digby 145 manuscript for John Fortescue-Aland, Lord Fortescue of Credan. Clermont quotes Hearne's diary statement that the 1714 edition was published from this copy without acknowledgment. Its present physical location and survival have not been verified.
notes: This is the documented intermediary behind the 1714 print, not the original Digby 145 witness and not a currently identified catalog object.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Rawlinson B.384
entities.description: Mid-seventeenth-century witness at ff. 42-68, copied in two hands and linguistically modernized. Plummer infers that it was copied from an unidentified shared text taken from Digby 145, not directly from Digby 145.

holding.notes: No exact current public catalog or Digital Bodleian object was verified on 2026-07-15.

notes: Plummer siglum R.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Ii.3.11
entities.description: Seventeenth-century witness at ff. 214-241. Plummer infers that it and Rawlinson B.384 were copied from one unidentified text taken from Digby 145; its copyist additionally used a separate unidentified text of the Yelverton textual type.

holding.notes: The ArchivesSearch route was blocked by a WAF and no CUDL object was verified on 2026-07-15.

notes: Plummer siglum Cb; the shelfmark's first component is capital I plus lowercase i.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Digby 145

scope.notes: Front and back matter are omitted from verbal encoding even though 412 PB milestones preserve the full page-image sequence.

subject.description: Target text through the base manuscript's loss

object.description: Work text through Chapter XIX at the point where the manuscript breaks off

scope.notes: Cotton supplies only the terminal passage in the printed base text; it was also collated across the work.
Clermont says he prints the 1714 text after further collation.
Clermont quotes Hearne's statement that he transcribed Sir Adrian Fortescue's manuscript; the transcript itself has not been independently inspected.
Hearne states that Lord Fortescue published the piece from his copy. This replaces a flattened direct edition-to-Digby edge.

subject.description: Inferred shared text behind R and Cb

scope.notes: Plummer says the shared text was taken from D2; the physical intermediary is unidentified.
Plummer excludes direct copying between R and Cb and infers their shared source.
This edge covers the principal D2-derived layer; the separate Y-type contribution has its own scoped edge.

subject.description: Material attributed by Plummer to a Y-type source
locators: titles of Chapters VIII, XII, and XIII

object.description: Corresponding material in an unidentified Y-type text

scope.notes: Textual affinity does not identify this source with surviving Add MS 48031 A.

rights.basis: The work was composed in the fifteenth century and printed no later than 1714; this excludes modern photography, catalog text, encodings, and contractual access terms.

rights.component: Complete repository XML, including inherited U-M text and markup
rights.basis: The exact rights boundary between contributor-owned repository material and copied U-M content is not established; the repository license alone cannot relicense upstream material.

rights.component: Repository-contributed software-like wrapping and original contributions, if any
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository asserts the Unlicense for software and source/binary forms. This is recorded only for contributor-owned original repository contributions, not as blanket permission for the complete XML or upstream content.
The current U-M item labels the item Public Domain and links the DPLA No Copyright - United States statement. This current assertion is preserved separately from the older XML-header warning.
The embedded header says copyright may apply where applicable and that other restrictions may govern distribution; it provides no item-specific open license.
notes: The discrepancy between this legacy generic statement and the current U-M item status is retained rather than silently reconciled.

rights.basis: Published in 1885; the edition is in the public domain in the United States.
Published in 1714; the edition is in the public domain in the United States.
Published in 1869; the edition is in the public domain in the United States.
Public download establishes access but is not itself an item-specific copyright license; provider terms may apply.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Laud Misc. 593 breaks off in Chapter XIX; Cotton Claudius A VIII preserves the ending
editorial_practices.representation: Laud is followed through p. 155, line 27, then Cotton supplies the rest through p. 157
editorial_practices.description: Plummer states the passage boundary and identifies the supplied witness.
editorial_practices.scope: Target pp. 109-157, especially p. 155 line 27 through p. 157

editorial_practices.representation: Selected departures and variants in notes rather than a full diplomatic apparatus
editorial_practices.description: Plummer collated all ten witnesses known to him while using Laud and then Cotton as passage-scoped base sources.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript punctuation, capitals, letterforms, and word division
editorial_practices.representation: Modern punctuation and capitalization; Gothic type for quotations as in Y; generally manuscript word division except the indefinite article
editorial_practices.description: Plummer says the edition is historical rather than narrowly philological and documents these interventions.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 387-page Plummer carrier and 412 scanned page images
editorial_practices.representation: Verbal encoding retains the title page and only pp. 109-157; other leaves survive as omitted PB milestones
editorial_practices.description: The XML's physical page-image map is much broader than its transcribed verbal content.

editorial_practices.representation: Level 1 TEI/OCR with linked page milestones and no corrections
editorial_practices.description: The header explicitly reports uncorrected OCR.
editorial_practices.scope: Retained title page and target pp. 109-157

evidence.citation: Pinned General Corpus XML for U-M CME item AEW3422.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Supports exact file identity, hashes, source edition, 2006 Level 1 OCR statement, 412-image carrier, selection boundary, title page, chapter headings, page milestones, and legacy rights warning.

evidence.citation: Structural inspection of the pinned aew3422 XML.
evidence.summary: Counts 23 DIV1 total, 20 BODY chapter DIV1, 20 BODY HEAD, 21 BODY P, and 412 PB; maps 131 FRONT, 50 BODY, and 231 BACK PB milestones and verifies the exact chapter/page boundaries.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, AEW3422.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Provides the current public item and persistent routes and the current Public Domain / No Copyright - United States rights label.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c8.
evidence.summary: Records the repository's Unlicense assertion, framed for software and source/binary forms; it does not establish ownership of upstream U-M content.

evidence.citation: Charles Plummer, ed., The Governance of England (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1885), complete scan.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the target on pp. 109-157, composition discussion, ten witnesses, sigla, base/supply boundary, collation, normalization, and prior printed editions.
evidence.locator: preface; introduction pp. 86-96; target pp. 109-157; critical notes including p. 167

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the complete cached Plummer 1885 scan and target boundaries.
evidence.summary: Confirms the title page, twenty numbered chapters on pp. 109-157, Laud/Cotton boundary at p. 155 line 27, and the location of Plummer's witness discussion.
evidence.locator: pp. 109-157 and critical note to p. 155 line 27

evidence.citation: John Fortescue-Aland, ed., The Difference between an Absolute and a Limited Monarchy (1714), complete scan.
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1714 title page, claim of transcription from a Bodleian manuscript and collation with three others, target pp. 1-147, and Adrian Fortescue colophon p. 148.

evidence.citation: Thomas Fortescue, Lord Clermont, ed., The Works of Sir John Fortescue, vol. 1 (1869), complete scan.
evidence.summary: On pp. 445-447 identifies Digby 145 as Sir Adrian Fortescue's 1532 copy; quotes Thomas Hearne's diary statement that he transcribed it about 1698 or 1699 and that Lord Fortescue published the 1714 piece from Hearne's copy; records the 1714 Laud, Digby 198, and Cotton collations and Clermont's further Lambeth 262 and Harley 542 collation; prints the target on pp. 449-474.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS Laud Misc. 593.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, full target as item 1, paper support, English origin, and current date 1500-1510.
notes: Official TEI catalog metadata pinned to Bodleian medieval-mss commit cd36dce9; it describes the target but contains no manuscript page images or work transcription.

The live medieval.bodleian catalog HTML returned an Anubis challenge to automated retrieval on 2026-07-15; the exact official TEI from Bodleian's public medieval-mss repository is cached at pinned commit cd36dce9 instead.

evidence.citation: Exact public-facsimile searches for the ten Plummer witnesses.
evidence.summary: A public complete Digby 145 surrogate was verified. Exact Digital Bodleian searches did not resolve Laud Misc. 593, Digby 198, or Rawlinson B.384; exact Lambeth LUNA searches did not resolve MS 262; current BL records supplied no usable full facsimiles for the four BL witnesses; and no CUDL object for Ii.3.11 was verified. These are dated negative searches, not proof that no digitization exists.
notes: Searches were performed at provider-specific official discovery services and recorded conservatively.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections image and scan requests.
evidence.summary: Provides the request route for non-public manuscript reproductions.

evidence.citation: British Library manuscript request and reference-services forms.
evidence.summary: Provides current physical-request and enquiry routes for BL manuscripts.

evidence.summary: Provides current remote research and low-resolution Special Collections scan guidance.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian terms of use.
evidence.summary: Provides provider terms governing Digital Bodleian images and metadata.

open_questions.question: What is the exact composition, revision, and intended-recipient sequence between the Lancastrian exile context and the extant 1471-1476 form associated with Edward IV?
next_steps: Compare the full textual recensions and current Fortescue scholarship.
Retain 1471-1476 as the supported extant-work range meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Do the Chapter-XVIII endings in Lambeth 262 and Digby 198 represent an earlier recension, deliberate abbreviation, or loss in a shared exemplar?
next_steps: Collate the endings and codicological transitions directly from reproductions.
Do not assert a stemmatic relation until that comparison is complete.

open_questions.question: What unidentified intermediary or intermediaries connect Digby 145 with Rawlinson B.384 and CUL Ii.3.11, and how did Yelverton-family readings enter the Cambridge text?
next_steps: Obtain images of Rawlinson B.384 and CUL Ii.3.11.
Perform passage-level collation and retain the relation as supported and explicitly mediated meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Can complete public or requestable digital facsimiles be obtained for the nine Plummer witnesses other than Digby 145?
next_steps: Use the recorded institutional enquiry and imaging routes.
Request whole manuscripts when feasible and map target folios inside each full reproduction.

open_questions.question: How should the current U-M Public Domain / No Copyright - US label be reconciled with the generic legacy copyright-may-apply warning embedded in the 2006 XML?
next_steps: Seek item-specific written clarification from U-M.
Preserve both dated assertions until clarified.
